techniques.name: Brute Force
techniques.description: Adversaries may use brute force techniques to gain access to accounts when passwords are unknown or when password hashes are obtained.
techniques.detection: Monitor authentication logs for multiple failed login attempts from a single source.

techniques.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter
techniques.description: Adversaries may abuse command and script interpreters to execute commands, scripts, or binaries.
techniques.detection: Monitor process creation for suspicious command-line arguments and script execution.
remediation: Monitor and log all command execution

techniques.name: Network Service Discovery
techniques.description: Adversaries may attempt to get a listing of services running on remote hosts and local network infrastructure devices.
techniques.detection: Monitor network traffic for port scanning behavior and unusual connection patterns.

techniques.name: Valid Accounts
techniques.description: Adversaries may obtain and abuse credentials of existing accounts as a means of gaining Initial Access, Persistence, Privilege Escalation, or Defense Evasion.
techniques.detection: Monitor login activity for unusual access patterns, times, or locations.

techniques.name: Process Injection
techniques.description: Adversaries may inject code into processes in order to evade process-based defenses as well as possibly elevate privileges.
techniques.detection: Monitor for suspicious process behavior including unexpected DLL loading and memory modifications.
remediation: Use endpoint detection and response tools

techniques.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
techniques.description: Adversaries may attempt to exploit a weakness in an Internet-facing host or system to initially access a network.
techniques.detection: Monitor web server logs for exploitation attempts including SQL injection and command injection.

techniques.name: Application Layer Protocol
techniques.description: Adversaries may communicate using OSI application layer protocols to avoid detection by blending in with existing traffic.
techniques.detection: Monitor network traffic for unusual application layer communications.
remediation: Block known C2 domains and IPs

techniques.name: Abuse Elevation Control Mechanism
techniques.description: Adversaries may circumvent mechanisms designed to control elevated privileges to gain higher-level permissions.
techniques.detection: Monitor for unauthorized privilege escalation attempts and sudo abuse.

techniques.name: Remote Services
techniques.description: Adversaries may use valid accounts to log into a service that accepts remote connections, such as SSH, RDP, or VNC.
techniques.detection: Monitor for unusual remote access patterns and connections from suspicious IPs.
remediation: Restrict remote access to specific IPs
Monitor and log all remote sessions

techniques.name: Network Denial of Service
techniques.description: Adversaries may perform Network Denial of Service attacks to degrade or block the availability of targeted resources.
techniques.detection: Monitor network traffic for volumetric anomalies and connection floods.

techniques.name: Indicator Removal
techniques.description: Adversaries may delete or modify artifacts generated on a host system to remove evidence of their presence.

sub_techniques.name: Clear Linux or Mac System Logs

techniques.detection: Monitor for log deletion and modification events.
remediation: Protect log files with proper permissions
Enable audit logging for file access

techniques.name: Create Account
techniques.description: Adversaries may create an account to maintain access to victim systems.
techniques.detection: Monitor for unexpected account creation events in authentication and system logs.

techniques.name: Impair Defenses
techniques.description: Adversaries may maliciously modify components of a victim environment to hinder or disable defensive mechanisms.
techniques.detection: Monitor for modifications to security tools, firewall rules, and logging configurations.

techniques.name: Scheduled Task/Job
techniques.description: Adversaries may abuse task scheduling functionality to facilitate initial or recurring execution of malicious code.
techniques.detection: Monitor for creation of new scheduled tasks or cron jobs.
remediation: Audit scheduled tasks and cron jobs regularly
Restrict who can create scheduled tasks
Use file integrity monitoring on crontab files

techniques.name: Server Software Component
techniques.description: Adversaries may abuse legitimate extensible development features of servers to establish persistent access to systems.
techniques.detection: Monitor web server logs for web shell access patterns and unusual POST requests.
remediation: Implement file integrity monitoring on web directories
Monitor for unusual web server file changes

techniques.name: Network Sniffing
techniques.description: Adversaries may passively sniff network traffic to capture information passing over the network.
techniques.detection: Monitor for network interfaces being placed in promiscuous mode.

techniques.name: OS Credential Dumping
techniques.description: Adversaries may attempt to dump credentials from the operating system and software to obtain account login information.
techniques.detection: Monitor for processes accessing credential stores and sensitive authentication files.

techniques.name: Ingress Tool Transfer
techniques.description: Adversaries may transfer tools or other files from an external system into a compromised environment.
techniques.detection: Monitor for file downloads using utilities like wget, curl, or certutil.

techniques.name: Unix Shell
techniques.description: Adversaries may abuse Unix shell commands and scripts for execution, including bash, sh, and other interpreters.
techniques.detection: Monitor for suspicious shell command execution patterns.
remediation: Restrict shell access to authorized users

techniques.name: Hijack Execution Flow
techniques.description: Adversaries may execute their own malicious payloads by hijacking the way operating systems run programs.
techniques.detection: Monitor for unusual library loading patterns and modifications to execution paths.
remediation: Use full path for binary execution
Restrict write access to system directories
